rationale: The cited paper appears only once in a grouped literature citation in the background section, where it is listed as an example of prior multicategory choice work on product bundles. The citing paper does not discuss Chung and Rao's model, findings, or methods in developing its own theory, hypotheses, or estimation approach. Instead, the citation serves a broad positioning function (prior work assuming compatibility) before contrasting to the current paper's incompatibility setting. Because no distinct functional role is assigned to this specific reference beyond background listing, the citation is mention-only.
